tests.outside_scope_reason: Summary mentions detecting pow2 from shl, umin, and select operations, but does not explicitly cover direct constant power-of-two literals as inputs to cttz/ctlz. This is a simpler case that tryGetLog2 should handle.

alive2.log:
----------------------------------------
define i32 @cttz_direct_constant_pow2() {
#0:
  %v = cttz i32 16, 0
  ret i32 %v
}
=>
define i32 @cttz_direct_constant_pow2() {
#0:
  ret i32 4
}
Transformation seems to be correct!

tests.source_program:
define i16 @ctlz_phi_node_pow2(i1 %cond, i16 %a, i16 %b) {
entry:
  br i1 %cond, label %if, label %else
if:
  %p2_a = shl i16 1, %a
  br label %merge
else:
  %p2_b = shl i16 1, %b
  br label %merge
merge:
  %phi = phi i16 [ %p2_a, %if ], [ %p2_b, %else ]
  %r = call i16 @llvm.ctlz.i16(i16 %phi, i1 false)
  ret i16 %r
}
tests.outside_scope_reason: Summary explicitly mentions select between pow2 operands, but phi nodes (which are structurally different control-flow merges) are not mentioned. tryGetLog2 may recognize phi nodes merging pow2 values from different branches.

alive2.src:
define i16 @ctlz_phi_node_pow2(i1 %cond, i16 %a, i16 %b) {
entry:
  br i1 %cond, label %if, label %else
if:
  %p2_a = shl i16 1, %a
  br label %merge
else:
  %p2_b = shl i16 1, %b
  br label %merge
merge:
  %phi = phi i16 [ %p2_a, %if ], [ %p2_b, %else ]
  %r = call i16 @llvm.ctlz.i16(i16 %phi, i1 false)
  ret i16 %r
}
alive2.tgt:
; ModuleID = '<stdin>'
source_filename = "<stdin>"

define i16 @ctlz_phi_node_pow2(i1 %cond, i16 %a, i16 %b) {
entry:
  br i1 %cond, label %if, label %else

if:                                               ; preds = %entry
  br label %merge

else:                                             ; preds = %entry
  br label %merge

merge:                                            ; preds = %else, %if
  %a.pn = phi i16 [ %a, %if ], [ %b, %else ]
  %r = sub nuw nsw i16 15, %a.pn
  ret i16 %r
}

; Function Attrs: nocallback nosync nounwind speculatable willreturn memory(none)
declare i16 @llvm.ctlz.i16(i16, i1 immarg) #0

attributes #0 = { nocallback nosync nounwind speculatable willreturn memory(none) }

tests.outside_scope_reason: Summary covers shl with constant base 2, but does not mention type extension operations (zext/sext) that preserve the power-of-two property across different bit widths. tryGetLog2 may trace through zext to detect the underlying pow2.

alive2.log:
----------------------------------------
define i32 @cttz_zext_pow2(i8 %x) {
#0:
  %p2 = shl i8 1, %x
  %ext = zext i8 %p2 to i32
  %r = cttz i32 %ext, 0
  ret i32 %r
}
=>
define i32 @cttz_zext_pow2(i8 %x) {
#0:
  %r = zext i8 %x to i32
  ret i32 %r
}
Transformation seems to be correct!
